text:
To find out how many miles Alisa and Stanley biked in total, we need to calculate the distance each of them biked separately and then add those distances together.

The formula to calculate distance is:
\[ \text{Distance} = \text{Speed} \times \text{Time} \]

For Alisa:
- Speed = 12 miles per hour
- Time = 4.5 hours
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Alisa}} = 12 \, \text{miles/hour} \times 4.5 \, \text{hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Alisa}} = 54 \, \text{miles} \]

For Stanley:
- Speed = 10 miles per hour
- Time = 2.5 hours
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Stanley}} = 10 \, \text{miles/hour} \times 2.5 \, \text{hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Stanley}} = 25 \, \text{miles} \]

Now, we add the distances together to find the total:
\[ \text{Total Distance} = \text{Distance}_{\text{Alisa}} + \text{Distance}_{\text{Stanley}} \]
\[ \text{Total Distance} = 54 \, \text{miles} + 25 \, \text{miles} \]
\[ \text{Total Distance} = 79 \, \text{miles} \]

So, Alisa and Stanley biked a total of 79 miles.
